state.gs_web_search_raw:
Here is the raw, specific, and uncensored material captured from the web search results, organized meticulously by your strategic queries to aid in trend mapping and audience resonance analysis.

---

### Query 1: PRS SE CE 24 reviews for live stage performance

*   **Weight & Stage Comfort:**
    *   Reviewers consistently highlight the guitar's lightweight body as a massive asset for long live sets. The **PRS SE CE 24 Standard Satin** is described as "feather light," weighing in at just under **2.8 kg (around 6.1 lbs)** to **7.3 lbs**. The maple-topped SE CE 24 is roughly **3.37 kg (7.4 lbs)**. 
    *   Gigging guitarists note it sits incredibly well on a strap without causing neck dive or shoulder strain, with one reviewer writing: *"Being light and comfortable, it is much less stress on my body over 3 hours of playing."*
*   **Neck Profile & Playability:**
    *   Features a **Wide Thin bolt-on maple neck** (with a 25-inch scale, 10-inch fretboard radius).
    *   Reviewers note the satin finish on the neck feels "very natural" and "silky smooth". The neck profile is described as a *"comfortable slinky kind of fairly slim C"* that is fast and friendly for shredding and alleviates hand fatigue/pain.
*   **Electronics & Sonic Versatility (The "Swiss Army Knife"):**
    *   Equipped with **PRS 85/15 "S" pickups** with a 3-way toggle and push/pull coil split on the tone pot.
    *   **Live Performance Tonality:** Reviewers claim it handles everything from pristine, ambient clean tones with chorus/reverb to high-gain Marshall-style Marshall leads and rootsy jangle.
    *   **Coil Tap vs. Split:** Multiple gigging reviews praise the coil tap, stating it sounds *"surprisingly much better than the usual split"*, delivering a *"convincing single coil bite... totally convincing on Rolling Stones tunes"* without getting overly thin, though some volume loss can occur (easily compensated with EQ).
    *   The pickups are noted for having a "noticeable brightness" and "hotter" output compared to US core pickups, which are sometimes described as sounding slightly "bitey" or "hollow" but cut through a live band mix exceptionally well.
*   **Tuning Stability on Stage:**
    *   Gigging players performing outdoor shows reported excellent tuning stability, even over 6-to-8-song sets with temperature changes.
    *   The **Stoptail (fixed bridge) version** with brass inserts offers massive tuning reliability, while the **Tremolo version** holds up well under standard use, though "vigorous vibrato abuse" can cause minor slippage.
*   **Price-to-Value Disruption:**
    *   Selling for **under $500** (€500) for the Standard Satin version and around **$669** for other models, it is hailed as a *"superb guitar for the money"* and *"the answer to the pain of skyrocketing prices"*. 

---

### Query 2: Summer music festival guitar gear trends 2024–2026

*   **The Direct-to-FOH (Direct-In) Stage Setup:**
    *   Stage volume management has become paramount. Guitarists are increasingly bypassing physical heavy amplifiers for **modeling pedals and amp-less rigs**. 
    *   This direct-signal trend minimizes stage feedback, accelerates setup/tear-down times, and reduces haulage weight. The focus is shifted from "honing a boutique tube tone" to "convenience and reliability".
*   **Miniaturization of Live Gear:**
    *   **Amp Modelers & Smart Tech:** Ultra-compact devices like the **IK Multimedia TONEX One**, **Fender Mustang Micro Plus**, and **Boss IR-2** are packing high-fidelity studio models into palm-sized formats. Musicians are touring globally with just their guitars and pocket-sized pedalboards.
    *   **LAVA ME Air:** Smart, ultra-thin 3/4-scale (36-inch) guitars integrated with internal microphone/pickup systems, customizable digital knobs, and bluetooth apps are trending for traveling and busking musicians.
*   **Boutique Customization & "Furniture" Gear:**
    *   **Johnny Foreigner Guitars:** Flagship models like "The Six" highlight a massive demand for hyper-customized setups (offering over 25 customization points from tone woods to finishes directly to the consumer in under 8 weeks starting at $1349).
    *   **Gear Furniture:** Brands like **Fender** (introducing the Deluxe Wooden Amp and Guitar Stands) and **On-Stage** (with the GWS5000 Guitar Workstation shelves) are leaning into the trend of guitar gear doubling as home lounge furniture, recognizing that most modern players are home creators with limited space.
*   **Aesthetic & Hardware Pivots:**
    *   The resurgence of **semi-hollow body guitars** (e.g., the Epiphone DG-335 Dave Grohl signature model).
    *   A massive surge in **offset body styles** (like Fender Jazzmasters) and **gold foil pickups** as players look for alternatives to traditional Stratocasters and Telecasters.

---

### Query 3: Guitarist stage presence trends social media 2024–2026

*   **The "Illusion of Flawlessness" vs. Algorithmic Burnout:**
    *   Social media (TikTok, Instagram) is shaping *how* guitarists play. Instead of continuous live sets, algorithms reward **15-to-60-second bite-sized clips**.
    *   This has created a standard of performance where videos are meticulously engineered to look "off-the-cuff" and spontaneous, but are actually the result of hundreds of takes. Guitarist Mateus Asato famously deactivated his Instagram in 2021 (returning with reflections later) due to feeling "lost inside the boxes" of short videos.
*   **The Homogenization of Guitar Playing:**
    *   Because algorithms reward replicating what works, guitarists are falling into a trap of **playing the same 4-bar chord progressions and lo-fi beats**. 
    *   The platforms prefer atmospheric, textured acoustic/electric backings or flashy, purposeless shredding. Conversational, dynamic guitar work suited for live stages does not clip well and is overlooked online.
*   **The "Why" Pivot & Community Building:**
    *   Content creators in 2025/2026 are shifting from "flashy playing" (which is noted as starting to die out unless you are in the top 1% of virtuosos) to **value-driven education and transparency**. 
    *   Guitarists like Damion Andy suggest a "5-to-1 content ratio" (80% free educational/tonal building content, 20% self-promotion/merch) to escape the burnout loop and build authentic, collaborative data-sharing communities.
*   **Physical Stage Presence (Live Venues):**
    *   To translate social media hype to a live stage, educators emphasize that **movement must be big and dramatic** to engage fans sitting hundreds of feet away. 
    *   Guitarists are urged to record themselves from the back of the venue during rehearsals to analyze their charisma, energy, and body language.

---

### Query 4: PRS Guitars marketing strategy for young musicians

*   **The PRS "Pulse Artist" Program (Grassroots Endorsement):**
    *   Recognizing that not every young player will reach the arena status of John Mayer or Carlos Santana, PRS launched the **Pulse Artist Program**.
    *   **How it Works:** Young, influential, "hometown hero" musicians apply through local authorized dealers. If accepted into the annual roster, they get:
        1. Exclusive discounts on PRS guitars, amps, and accessories.
        2. Exposure on a dedicated Pulse Artist roster on the official PRS website.
        3. Broad amplification on PRS social media platforms and custom editorial content.
    *   **Requirements:** Intermediate/advanced skills, a strong online presence/social media engagement, and a preference for the PRS brand.
*   **Humanizing the Brand & Emotional Connections:**
    *   PRS focuses on establishing "relationships" rather than transactional sales. Their marketing messaging centers around the **"musical journey"** of the player.
    *   They prioritize "humanizing" their guitars by highlighting the factory workers and artisans behind the builds, attempting to forge a visceral, emotional reaction in young buyers.
*   **Modernization of the PR Team:**
    *   In 2022, PRS appointed Jared Welling as PR Manager (reporting to Judy Schaefer, Director of Marketing). Welling's background includes Sony Music analytics and marketing for mainstream acts like *The Chainsmokers*, signaling a pivot toward modern data-driven digital campaigns and festival-scale artist integrations.

---

### Query 5: Why are electric guitars trending with Gen Z musicians

*   **Digital Discovery Platforms (TikTok & Instagram):**
    *   According to industry research, **40% of Gen Z guitar players discover instruments through TikTok and Instagram**. 60% of new players cite online video content as their primary motivation for picking up the instrument.
    *   They consume short-form, visually appealing guitar content, establishing an instant connection with players rather than traditional brick-and-mortar music stores.
*   **The Post-Pandemic Hobbyist Wave:**
    *   The COVID-19 pandemic catalyzed a massive surge in guitar adoption. Fender estimated that **16 million Americans learned to play during the first two years of the pandemic**, with a vast majority being young, diverse, and tech-savvy. 
    *   A massive portion of this wave consisted of young women discovering the instrument.
*   **Seamless Integration with Home Recording (DAWs):**
    *   Unlike acoustic guitars, electric guitars integrate seamlessly into digital audio workstations (DAWs) for home studio setups. 
    *   Gen Z musicians favor electrics for their **direct line-in connectivity, low-noise recording options, and digital processing versatility**, allowing them to produce professional-quality bedroom pop, rock, or trap tracks easily.
*   **Genre-Blending & Sonic Versatility:**
    *   Gen Z does not restrict the electric guitar to classic rock or metal. They use the instrument's vast tonal options (from clean, sparkling ambient textures to heavy distortion) for cross-genre collaborations. 
    *   Iconic modern artists have helped popularize this; for instance, extreme virtuoso **Nuno Bettencourt touring with Rihanna** introduced the electric guitar to massive pop audiences.
*   **Modern "Guitar Heroes" & Niche Brands:**
    *   The traditional, untouchable "Guitar God" archetype of the '70s and '80s has faded. Instead, Gen Z looks to highly relatable, visually stunning online icons like **Tim Henson (Polyphia)**, **Ichika Nito**, and **Marcin Patrzałek**.
    *   This has propelled highly specific guitar styles to the mainstream, notably **nylon-string acoustic-electrics** (such as Henson's signature Ibanez TOD10N) and **headless guitars** (such as Strandberg).

## Trend Overview & Trajectory

The landscape of live music and guitar performance is undergoing a major structural shift driven by digital-first creators, hyper-portable technology, and price-to-value disruption. This trend is currently **accelerating rapidly** and is projected to have sustained staying power through 2026. 

The traditional "Guitar God" archetype, characterized by heavy physical amplifier stacks and exclusionary virtuosity, has been replaced by a more accessible, genre-fluid, and tech-driven ethos. The modern guitarist's ecosystem is split between ultra-curated, short-form digital spaces (TikTok/Instagram) and highly streamlined, direct-to-front-of-house (FOH) live stage setups. High-quality, lightweight, and versatile gear—such as the PRS SE CE 24 Standard Satin (priced aggressively under $500)—is dominating the market, proving that entry-level barriers are collapsing. This is a highly resilient movement fueled by a massive wave of post-pandemic players who prioritize convenience, digital integration, and functional reliability over legacy brand prestige.

## Key Entities and Cultural Narrative

The cultural conversation is anchored by a tension between digital perfection and raw, authentic live performance.

*   **The Digital Paradox:** Gen Z is discovering the guitar primarily through social media, with **40% of young players finding instruments via TikTok and Instagram**, and 60% citing online video as their primary motivation. This has birthed highly visual, genre-fluid online icons like **Tim Henson (Polyphia)**, **Ichika Nito**, and **Marcin Patrzałek**, as well as mainstream crossovers like extreme virtuoso **Nuno Bettencourt touring with Rihanna**. 
*   **Algorithmic Burnout:** However, the pressure to deliver "flawless" 15-to-60-second clips has led to severe burnout (typified by virtuoso Mateus Asato temporarily deactivating his social media). This is sparking a cultural pivot away from "flashy shredding" and toward transparency, value-driven education, and genuine community building.
*   **The Live "Direct-In" Revolution:** On stage, the narrative centers on "convenience over boutique tone." Heavy physical amps are being abandoned for ultra-compact modeling tech (e.g., **IK Multimedia TONEX One**, **Fender Mustang Micro Plus**, **Boss IR-2**) to eliminate stage volume, accelerate festival turnarounds, and reduce travel strain.
*   **Democratic Disruption:** Legacy brand **PRS Guitars** is successfully humanizing its brand and capturing the youth market. By prioritizing grassroots endorsement through its **Pulse Artist Program** (supporting "hometown heroes" rather than just arena acts) and hiring PR talent with mainstream pop analytics backgrounds, PRS is aligning directly with the values of diverse, young, and self-produced musicians.

## Marketing Opportunity Analysis

### 1. Position "Affordable/Lightweight" as the Ultimate Creative Freedom
Marketers should leverage the physical and financial accessibility of instruments like the PRS SE CE 24 Standard Satin to target bedroom producers and touring musicians alike. 
*   **The Angle:** Contrast the "pain of skyrocketing prices" and heavy, back-breaking gear with the liberation of high-performance, ultra-lightweight (under 6.1 lbs), and budget-friendly equipment. 
*   **Execution:** Create content campaigns featuring fast-paced, "run-and-gun" touring musicians setting up an entire stage-ready rig from a single backpack. Emphasize that "pro-grade" no longer requires a road crew or a second mortgage.

### 2. Capture the "Direct-to-DAW" & Bedroom Pop Producer Market
With Gen Z utilizing electric guitars for cross-genre bedroom pop, trap, and ambient music, marketing must move past traditional "classic rock" imagery.
*   **The Angle:** Highlight seamless integration with Digital Audio Workstations (DAWs) and direct-in modeling rigs.
*   **Execution:** Partner with young, diverse home producers for "Plug & Play" challenges on TikTok/Reels, showing how easily a versatile guitar with coil-tap capabilities (like the PRS SE CE 24) can transition from a lo-fi beat element to a crisp single-coil pop lick, straight into a laptop.

### 3. Lean into the "Anti-Gatekeeping" & Educational Content Pivot
As audiences grow weary of over-engineered, flawless 15-second shred videos, there is a massive opportunity to champion transparency and "real-world" preparation.
*   **The Angle:** Align the brand with the "Why" pivot—fostering community, showing the "imperfect" creative process, and building stage confidence.
*   **Execution:** Develop a campaign around the concept of "Rehearsing for the Back of the Room." Create educational content featuring grassroots artists (such as PRS Pulse Artists) teaching players how to translate online bedroom skills into physical, high-energy stage presence, utilizing a "5-to-1" content strategy that prioritizes value and utility over self-promotion.

## Target Audience and Behavioral Insights

The target demographic of beginner and intermediate guitarists aged 18–35 exhibits distinct purchasing behaviors, pain points, and lifestyle associations that set them apart from traditional legacy buyers:

*   **Versatility Over Niche Categorization:** This demographic prioritizes a single "all-rounder" instrument capable of spanning multiple genres—such as rock, pop, blues, worship, and metal—over highly specialized, single-tone instruments. They heavily favor flexible hardware configurations, specifically valuing highly responsive coil-splitting options that deliver convincing single-coil tones alongside full-force humbuckers.
*   **Playability and Physical Comfort:** Key physical pain points for developing players include hand fatigue, tuning instability, and string-bending resistance. This audience responds strongly to ergonomic features that facilitate easier play:
    *   **The 25" Scale Length:** Recognized as a "sweet spot" that reduces string tension compared to 25.5" scale lengths, making bends easier while preventing string buzz.
    *   **Flatter Fretboard Radii:** Flatter profiles (such as 10" or wider) are highly favored over vintage 7.25" or 9.5" curves, preventing notes from "fretting out" or choking during solos.
    *   **Tuning Reliability:** Aspiring players seek "set-it-and-forget-it" hardware to maximize practice time and eliminate constant retuning.
*   **Aesthetic and Visual Identity:** Younger players reject dated, traditional finishes (like classic sunbursts) in favor of modern, stage-ready aesthetics, including metallic satin finishes, turquoise, and pastel options.
*   **DIY Culture and Modularity:** There is a strong subset of the 18–35 demographic engaged in DIY guitar modification. They actively seek out reliable "modding platforms" featuring bolt-on necks, which allow for easy neck adjustments, shimming, and hardware upgrades.
*   **Peer Trust and Experiential Purchase Journeys:** This audience actively distrusts traditional corporate advertising. They rely heavily on peer reviews, user-generated content (UGC), and hands-on, low-pressure trial environments (such as festival booths and interactive "Instrument Playgrounds").

---

## Product Landscape and Competitive Context

The mid-range electric guitar market is highly competitive, sitting primarily in the $450 to $800 price bracket. The PRS SE CE 24 family operates in this tier with two distinct variations: the standard maple-topped **PRS SE CE 24** (approx. $699 USD) and the stripped-back, all-mahogany **PRS SE CE 24 Standard Satin** (approx. $499 USD).

### Main Competitors
*   **Fender Player II Series Stratocaster/Telecaster (~$799):** The standard industry benchmark. However, they feature a stiffer 25.5" scale length, a rounder 9.5" fretboard radius, and are often perceived as less versatile out-of-the-box compared to the coil-split PRS system.
*   **Squier Classic Vibe Series (~$450–$499):** A highly popular budget alternative, but often viewed as a vintage-spec replica rather than a modern, high-performance workhorse.
*   **Yamaha Pacifica 612 / 112V (~$300–$780):** Highly respected for value, though sometimes lacking the premium lifestyle brand appeal associated with PRS.
*   **Schecter PT Standard / C-6 Deluxe (~$500–$600):** Popular among heavier players due to flatter 14" fretboard radii, but lacking the broader multi-genre appeal of the PRS SE line.

### Market Position & Key Differentiators
*   **The PRS Dedicated Factory Advantage:** Unlike competitors (such as Fender, Squier, Epiphone, and Schecter) that are frequently contract-manufactured alongside one another in massive third-party facilities, the PRS SE line is built in a dedicated, custom facility in Indonesia. This setup yields vastly superior quality control, flawless out-of-the-box fretwork, and construction consistency that rivals instruments costing two to three times as much.
*   **Ergonomics:** The shallow "violin" body carve of the SE CE 24 is highly praised for forearm comfort, outperforming older, sharper USA Core designs. Additionally, the guitar is lightweight (typically around 7.35 lbs / 3.34 kg), making it physically ideal for long standing sessions.

### Common Misconceptions & Performance Hurdles
*   **The "Bare Lumber" Satin Neck Finish:** While some players appreciate the speed of the satin-finished maple neck, others complain that the very light finish feels dry, cheap, or comparable to entry-level budget models.
*   **Stock Pickup Performance (85/15 "S"):** While adequate for lead lines, the stock pickups are criticized by some reviewers as "bitey and hollow" compared to American-made counterparts, occasionally introducing midrange clutter when playing full chords through high-gain systems.
*   **Lack of Locking Tuners:** Despite high overall stability, the omission of stock locking tuners on a mid-range "performance" guitar is a common complaint for players looking for rapid restringing.

---

## Strategic Opportunities & Key Message Validation

### 1. Validate the "Indestructible Summer Festival Workhorse" Narrative
*   **The Insight:** Aspiring gigging musicians need an ultra-reliable, road-worthy instrument that can withstand the physical chaos of travel, outdoor elements, and live performances without falling apart or dropping tune.
*   **Why it Works:** Synthesizing the physical durability of the bolt-on maple neck (which prevents catastrophic headstock breaks common in set-neck designs) with the PRS patented tremolo's exceptional tuning stability creates a highly compelling "road warrior" profile. 
*   **Campaign Execution:** Pitch the SE CE 24 as the ultimate "grab-and-go" festival companion. Highlight its lightweight footprint (7.35 lbs) and the inclusion of a high-quality gig bag. Visually showcase the modern "Satin Gold" finish, which reflects beautifully and "glows" under low-light stage conditions without appearing gaudy.

### 2. Leverage the "Ultimate Modding Platform" Angle for DIYers
*   **The Insight:** The 18–35 demographic loves customization and personalization. They view their mid-range guitar purchases as evolving platforms rather than static instruments.
*   **Why it Works:** Bolt-on necks are mechanically modular, allowing for easy neck angle adjustments (shimming), fret maintenance, or replacements. While the raw satin neck and stock 85/15 "S" pickups are occasionally viewed as minor drawbacks out of the box, they can be recontextualized as the perfect canvas for personalization.
*   **Campaign Execution:** Position the SE CE 24 as a high-quality foundation—boasting world-class fretwork, a premium body carve, and elite bridge hardware—that is fully ready to be customized with aftermarket locking tuners or boutique pickups as the player grows.

### 3. Move Beyond Technical Specs to Experiential "Demo Days"
*   **The Insight:** Guitarists in this bracket are highly experiential and prioritize authentic peer validation over corporate advertising.
*   **Why it Works:** Standard spec sheets do not convey the tactile benefits of the 25" scale length, the comfortable violin carve, or the highly responsive coil-split electronics.
*   **Campaign Execution:** Deploy interactive "Instrument Playgrounds" at regional summer music festivals and coordinate localized retail "Demo Days" featuring exclusive single-day discounts. Utilize authentic user-generated content (UGC) and partner with local, indie-level festival guitarists to tell behind-the-scenes stories of prepping the guitar for real gigs.

---

## Key Research Gaps/Next Steps

To optimize future marketing campaigns, the following areas require further investigation:
*   **Consumer Sentiment on Fretboard Scent:** Investigate if the reported "strong chemical aroma" coming from the fretboard out of the gig bag is an isolated incident or a widespread quality control issue with current adhesive/oil treatments.
*   **Pickup Upgrade Preferences:** Conduct research into which specific aftermarket pickups are most commonly installed by players who swap out the stock 85/15 "S" units, helping to tailor future hardware bundles or modification guides.
*   **Demographic Split on Finish Preferences:** Gather quantitative data on the sales performance of the Standard Satin ($499) versus the Maple Veneer ($699) models among the 18–35 demographic to determine if price or visual aesthetic is the primary driver of purchase.

    *   With 40% of Gen Z players discovering instruments on TikTok and Instagram <cite source="src-28" />, standard corporate advertising is less effective than short-form, visually appealing user-generated content.
    *   Programs like the PRS Pulse Artist Program perfectly align with this culture, offering grassroots endorsements to intermediate players with strong online communities rather than exclusively sponsoring unreachable arena rock stars <cite source="src-24" />.

Feature real players and tech influencers upgrading the $499 Standard Satin model with aftermarket locking tuners and boutique pickups, framing the world-class fretwork and bolt-on neck as a premium "grow-with-you" platform <cite source="src-39" /> <cite source="src-57" />. Use copy like: *"Built to play. Born to customize."*
4.  **Festival Stage Aesthetic Showcase:** Visually highlight the modern finish options (such as Satin Gold and pastel shades) in low-light, outdoor festival environments. Emphasize how these metallic and satin finishes glow naturally on stage without looking gaudy, establishing them as the modern alternative to dated legacy sunbursts <cite source="src-39" /> <cite source="src-51" />.

state.visual_direction:
# Visual Direction Brief: PRS SE CE 24 "Festival Ready" Campaign

## 1. Mood & Tone
The campaign visualizes the electric energy of the summer music festival season, viewed through a gritty, authentic, "anti-gatekeeping" lens. The mood is high-energy, DIY, and unapologetically modern. Rather than polished, sterile arena-rock perfection, we are capturing the raw, humid, backstage-to-onstage reality of indie artists. It should feel lived-in, immediate, and sweat-drenched—balancing the adrenaline of a live outdoor performance with the clever, utilitarian pride of a musician who travels light and plays hard.

## 2. Colour Palette
We will reject dusty, vintage sepia tones in favor of a vibrant, high-contrast, modern festival palette:
*   **Stage Satin Gold (Primary Accent):** A warm, metallic, glowing gold that mimics the low-gloss satin finish of the guitar catching stage lights.
*   **Dusk Violet (Background/Shadows):** A deep, rich, warm purple-blue representing the magic hour of outdoor festival sets.
*   **Neon Amp Green (Highlights):** A sharp, electric, digital-leaning green to nod to the "Direct-to-DAW" and portable modeling tech.
*   **Raw Asphalt (Neutrals):** Gritty, textured dark grays and charcoal off-blacks representing stage cases, cables, and festival pavement.

## 3. Recurring Visual Motifs
*   **The "Rig-in-a-Backpack" Flatlay:** The PRS SE CE 24 resting alongside ultra-compact digital modelers, coiled patch cables, a laptop, and a single lightweight gig bag.
*   **The Golden Hour Halo:** Natural, warm outdoor stage flare catching the headstock and body contours, emphasizing the tactile satin finish.
*   **Direct-In Patching:** Graphic, stylized line overlays or glowing cables connecting the guitar directly to minimal digital interfaces, visually representing "No Backline."

## 4. Brand Visual Cues
*   **The PRS Silhouette:** The iconic PRS double-cutaway body and trademark bird fretboard inlays must always be crisp and recognizable, even in low-light or stylized treatments.
*   **The Bolt-On Joint:** Subtle close-up framing focusing on the mechanical beauty of the 4-bolt neck joint—symbolizing indestructible road-readiness.
*   **Natural Textures:** Avoid hyper-glossy studio renders. The guitar should show subtle, realistic signs of being played: light dust from the festival grounds, fingerprints on the hardware, and the soft, non-reflective sheen of the Satin finish.

## 5. Recommended Style Families
To match the diverse range of ad copy tones, we will avoid relying solely on photorealism:
*   **Style A: Gritty Realism (For *Your Back vs. Your Setlist*):** High-contrast, documentary-style photography. Feels like a real behind-the-scenes snapshot captured on a high-end mobile phone at a festival.
*   **Style B: Collage / Sticker-Meme (For *Me packing a full half-stack...*):** A playful, high-energy mix of cut-out photography, hand-drawn digital arrows, bold typographic callouts, and sticker-style graphics highlighting gear weights.
*   **Style C: Minimalist Graphic / Split-Screen (For *One Instrument. Every Single Vibe.*):** A clean, split-frame illustration or vector-photo hybrid showing the guitar body in the center, with the left half of the frame reflecting "clean/glassy funk" (cool pastel tones) and the right half reflecting "heavy high-gain" (hot, fiery red-orange tones).
*   **Style D: Cinematic Stage-Light (For *Under the Lights...*):** Moody, atmospheric, low-key lighting focused heavily on product design, where the Satin Gold finish acts as the primary light-reflective surface against a dark, hazy, outdoor festival stage background.
